articles.title: INTRODUCING REPLIT DESIGN MODE
articles.summary: Replit's Design Mode is powered by Google's new Gemini 3 model. It enables users to create interactive mockups and static websites in under two minutes through natural language commands. The feature serves both as a rapid prototyping tool for designers and product managers, and as a faster alternative to website builders like Wix or Squarespace for creating deployable sites. Users can convert their front-end designs into full-stack applications with backend functionality by clicking "Convert to App." The entire development process happens within Replit's workspace.

articles.title: GOOGLE AI MODE IS STILL TESTING SHOWING ADS IN SEARCH RESULTS
articles.summary: Google is testing ads in its Gemini-powered AI Mode search results, placing sponsored cards at the bottom of pages rather than at the top like traditional searches. The company confirmed these intermittent ads are part of ongoing tests announced in May, with no new updates to AI Mode's ad distribution currently. Organic link cards now appear within Gemini's answer above the ads, and unlike traditional search, there's no option to hide sponsored results in AI Mode.

articles.title: AI TOOK CENTRE STAGE AT ADOBE MAX LA 2025
articles.summary: Adobe MAX LA 2025 showcased Adobe's push to make AI a powerful assistant rather than a replacement, integrating conversational agents across Photoshop, Firefly, and Express to handle repetitive tasks through plain-language commands. Major updates included the advanced Firefly Image Model 5, refined generative tools in Creative Cloud, new video features like AI Object Mask, and enterprise solutions such as GenStudio and Firefly Foundry—aimed at speeding up workflows while keeping creative control firmly with humans.

articles.title: GENERATIVE UI FROM GEMINI 3 PRO
articles.summary: Google's Gemini 3 Pro introduces Generative UI, where AI creates custom interfaces on the fly for individual users. Test users preferred these over traditional websites 90% of the time. Human designers currently edge out AI designs by just 56% to 43%, but this narrow margin is expected to disappear by late 2026 as AI capabilities double roughly every seven months while human design skills stagnate. Google plans to roll out Generative UI to its five billion search users, marking a shift from expensive, permanent interfaces toward cheap, disposable, individualized interactions generated instantly for specific user needs.

articles.title: HUMAN ENGINEERING
articles.summary: Human engineering is a multidisciplinary field focused on designing systems, technologies, and environments that make correct actions intuitive and errors difficult, rather than relying on training or willpower. This article illustrates this through everyday examples like stovetop configurations and "Norman Doors," demonstrating how poor spatial compatibility and discoverability lead to common mistakes that stem from design failures rather than human error. By applying principles such as natural mapping, affordances, and constraints, human engineering reduces cognitive load and creates systems where ordinary human performance produces excellent results at minimal cost.

articles.title: QUALITY OF DESIGN EDUCATION "LOWER THAN IT WAS" SAY DESIGNERS
articles.summary: Design education has deteriorated: it's more expensive, less connected to industry, and leaves graduates unprepared and in debt. Too many under-resourced courses lack real-world experience, sustainability training, and adequately supported teachers, suggesting that many skills can now be self-taught and that the traditional university model may need to be rebuilt entirely.

articles.title: BETTER MARKETING DECISIONS 10X FASTER
articles.summary: GoMarble is an AI agent that analyzes your paid marketing and gives instant insights, creative analyses, and expert recommendations.

articles.title: FREE ONLINE IMAGE CONVERTER
articles.summary: Convert images between formats, locally in your browser, no uploads, 100% securely and privately.

articles.title: 12 TOP DESIGNERS TELL US HOW THEY MADE IT
articles.summary: Twelve established designers, including Philippe Starck and Yinka Ilori, candidly share how they broke into the industry, revealing stories of patience, side jobs, and serendipitous connections that launched their careers. Many express concern about today's design landscape, citing reduced funding, expensive trade shows, and inadequate education as major barriers for emerging designers compared to when they started. Despite these challenges, the designers note that social media and independent approaches have created new pathways, with Instagram facilitating collaborations and designers increasingly bypassing traditional trade shows to take control of their own work presentation.

articles.title: WHEN NATURAL LANGUAGE FEELS UNNATURAL
articles.summary: ChatGPT popularized natural language as a consumer interface in late 2023, but chat's dominance as the primary AI interaction method may be temporary, much like command-line interfaces before graphical user interfaces emerged. While natural language excels for knowledge work and coding, where ambiguity is tolerable, it struggles with precision tasks, leading to unwanted rewrites and misinterpretations that reveal chat as an incomplete interface layer. The field awaits its "Mother of All Demos" moment—a breakthrough that integrates multimodal inputs, contextual affordances, and dynamic interfaces into a new interaction grammar beyond simple text prompts.

articles.title: NINETY YEARS OF PENGUIN: HOW TYPOGRAPHY BUILT A PUBLISHING ICON
articles.summary: Penguin's identity has always been rooted in typography, evolving from early consistent paperbacks to Jan Tschichold's 1940s philosophy of clarity, structure, and reader-centered design. Across decades—from Gill Sans modernism to Helvetica's international style to later post-modern experimentation—Penguin has adapted to cultural and technological change while preserving its core values of clarity, consistency, and respectful communication, showing how typography can shape both reading and culture itself.

articles.title: WHY 85% OF AI PROJECTS FAIL (AND HOW UX CAN PREVENT THESE COSTLY MISTAKES)
articles.summary: 85% of AI projects fail primarily because they lack valid use cases that address real user needs rather than just business objectives.

articles.title: LONDON'S NIGHTMARISH CHRISTMAS MURAL SHOWS WHAT HAPPENS WHEN AI REPLACES DESIGNERS
articles.summary: A London developer used generative AI to create a Christmas banner for a Kingston-upon-Thames development, resulting in a disturbing mural featuring distorted faces, malformed snowmen, and chimeric creatures that locals compared to "Lovecraftian horror."

articles.title: THIS FOOTBALL TEAM'S NEW LOGO IS PURE FAN SERVICE (AND I'M NOT MAD)
articles.summary: Iowa State has revived its beloved “Cynado” tornado-mascot logo with a modern refresh, delighting fans who hope it replaces the unpopular I-State wordmark permanently.
